content.full_text:
October 31, 1989


Andrew Sargent, Campaign Manager
Committee to Elect Kay Bara
1 Peppertree Drive
Rancho Palos Verdes, CA 90274

                                        Re:  Your Request for Advice
                                        Our File No. A-89[illegible]

Dear Mr. Sargent:

     You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided to
you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

     Your letter, dated September 28, 1989, correctly summarizes my
advice.  Loans are subject to the same limitations as other monetary
and non-monetary contributions and you may not make loans or
contributions aggregating more than $1,000 per fiscal year to a
candidate.  Although you have also correctly summarized my advice that
the loan you made to Ms. Bara should be shown coming in on Schedule B
of Form 490 and returned on Schedule E, we have reconsidered that
advice.   The committee should disclose receipt of the loan on Part 1
of Schedule B and reflect the return on Part 2 of Schedule B.  This is
necessary to properly reflect the committee's outstanding loan balance
on the summary page.

     Expenses which were paid directly by the candidate should be
disclosed as monetary contributions on Schedule A and as expenditures
on Schedule E.  Expenditures of $100 or more must be itemized, but
expenditures under $100 can be lumped together on line 2 of the
Summary at the bottom of Schedule E.

     Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have any additional
questions.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel
                                        
                                        By:  Wayne P. Imberi
                                             Political Reform Consultant


1_____Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000,
et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of
the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

October 2, 1989


Andrew Sargent
Campaign Manager
Committee to Elect Kay Bara
1 Peppertree Drive
Rancho Palos Verdes, CA  90274

Re:  Letter No. 89-567

Dear Mr. Sargent:

     We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on October 2, 1989. Your letter
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division for response. If you have any questions, you may contact
that division directly at (916) 322-5662.

     If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response will be released after it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

     You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

                                   Sincerely,


                                   [illegible]
                                   Kathryn E. Donovan
                                   General Counsel

KED:plh:confadv1


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

ELECT KAY BARA

COMMITTEE TO ELECT KAY BARA
1 Peppertree Drive
Rancho Palos Verdes, CA 90274
(213) 541-1914

OVER-
DEVELOPMENT

RPV CITY COUNCIL

September 28, 1989

California Fair Political Practices Committee
428 J Street
Sacramento, CA 95814
Attn. Wayne Imberi

Dear Mr. Imberi:

This letter confirms our discussion today concerning how to handle the errors I made when we
were starting the campaign organization. As we discussed, I did not realize a loan was counted
toward the $1,000 contribution limit. I made a second loan of $1,000 to the Committee which
is in technical violation. In addition, the candidate paid for several expenditures personally.

Please be assured we now understand the new complex regulations and will insure all future
actions are in full compliance. I apologize for the errors and they were my mistakes. I take full
responsibility for the violations. Per your instructions I have taken the following actions.

Loan to Committee in Excess of $1,000.00

The Receipt of the loans are shown on Schedule B. The return of the excess loan is listed on
Schedule E.

Expenses paid for directly by the Candidate.

We have included the amount expended on Schedule A as a direct cash contribution by the
candidate. We have included the amounts expended on Schedule E as expenditures.

I wish to thank you for you assistance. Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or
further guidance.

Sincerely yours,

Andrew Sergeant
Campaign Manager
